questions.question: Choose the one word substitute for the below sentence. Emily asked her friend, a person who has never been married called?
questions.explanation: D) Bachelor (noun) – A man who has never been married अविवाहित पुरुष • Single (adjective/noun) – Not married, but can be used generally without stressing “never married” अविवाहित • Celibate (noun/adjective) – A person who abstains from marriage or sexual relations ब्रह्मचारी • Widower (noun) – A man whose wife has died विधुर

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentence parts to form a meaningful and grammatically correct paragraph: P. He heated a bar of steel in the forge until it glowed a bright, brilliant orange. Q. He hammered the hot metal on the anvil, shaping it into the form he desired. R. He tempered the finished piece, dipping it in oil to give it strength and durability. S. The forged object was a strong, beautiful piece, born of fire and brute force.
questions.explanation: A) P, Q, S, R He heated a bar of steel in the forge until it glowed a bright, brilliant orange He hammered the hot metal on the anvil, shaping it into the form he desired The forged object was a strong, beautiful piece, born of fire and brute force He tempered the finished piece, dipping it in oil to give it strength and durability सबसे पहले P आएगा क्योंकि process heating से शुरू होता है। Steel को forge में गरम किया जाता है जब तक वह bright orange न हो जाए। बिना गरम किए metal को shape नहीं किया जा सकता, इसलिए शुरुआत P से होगी। इसके बाद Q आएगा क्योंकि heating के बाद hot metal को anvil पर hammer से shape दिया जाता है। Shaping हमेशा heating के बाद होता है, इसलिए Q logically P के बाद आएगा। फिर S आएगा क्योंकि shaping के बाद forged object का form तैयार हो चुका है। S उस forged object का description देता है — strong और beautiful piece। यह shaping के immediate result को बताता है। अंत में R आएगा क्योंकि tempering final strengthening process है। “He tempered the finished piece” दिखाता है कि object तैयार हो चुका है और अब उसे strength और durability देने के लिए oil में dip किया जा रहा है। इसलिए R अंत में fit बैठता है। First comes P because the process begins with heating. The bar of steel is heated in the forge until it glows bright orange. Without heating, the metal cannot be shaped, so this naturally forms the starting point of the paragraph. Next comes Q because after the metal becomes hot, it is hammered on the anvil to give it the desired shape. Shaping is the step that follows heating, so Q logically comes after P. Then comes S because once the metal has been forged into shape, it can be described as a strong and beautiful piece born of fire and force. This sentence highlights the appearance and nature of the forged object after shaping. Finally, R comes because tempering is the last strengthening step. “He tempered the finished piece” shows that the object is complete in form and is now being treated to increase its strength and durability. Therefore, R fits at the end as the concluding technical step.

questions.question: Choose the odd one out.
questions.explanation: D) Knit – Pronounced differently; it means to make fabric by interlocking yarn बुनना • Knight – Pronounced like “night”; a medieval soldier शूरवीर • Night – The time of darkness after sunset रात • Nite – Informal spelling of “night” अनौपचारिक रूप

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word “Exacting.”
questions.explanation: A) Exacting (adjective) – Making great demands on one's skill, attention, or other resources; rigorous and tough. (कठिन/सख्त) Antonym: Lenient (adjective) – (Of a person or a punishment) more merciful or tolerant than expected; not strict. (उदार/नरम) • Lucidly (adverb): In a way that is easy to understand or expressed clearly. (स्पष्ट रूप से) • Laconic (adjective): (Of a person, speech, or style of writing) using very few words; concise. (मितभाषी) • Languid (adjective): Displaying or having a disinclination for physical exertion or effort; slow and relaxed. (सुस्त)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the word: Hegemony
questions.explanation: A) Hegemony (noun) – dominance or leadership of one group over others आधिपत्य / प्रभुत्व Antonym:Subordination (noun) – being under authority or control अधीनता • Equality (noun) – state in which no group dominates; all are on the same level समानता • Leadership (noun) – act of leading or guiding others नेतृत्व • Authority (noun) – power or right to control or command अधिकार

questions.question: Choose the option that has an error in the sentence. When asked why students had enrolled in the geometry class, 85% of them saw it as essentials.
options: When asked why students had enrolled.
85% of them saw it as essentials.
questions.explanation: C) 'essentials' के बदले 'essential' होगा क्योंकि as के बाद यहाँ adjective चाहिए, न कि plural noun; वाक्य का अर्थ है कि वे इसे आवश्यक मानते थे, न कि आवश्यक वस्तुएँ। • 'essential' will be used instead of 'essentials' because after ‘as’ we need an adjective complement, not a plural noun; Like— They saw it as important, not importants.

questions.question: Identify the grammatical error in the sentence below. The borrower must return the book will done with reading.
questions.explanation: D) “the book will” के बदले “the book when” का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँ time clause व्यक्त करना है (जब पढ़ना पूरा हो जाए), न कि future modal ‘will’। जैसे — Return the book when you are done with reading. • “the book when” will be used instead of “the book will” because a time clause is required to show the point at which the action of returning happens; future auxiliary “will” is not used after when to express a future event. For example — Come to me when you finish your work.

questions.question: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: A) The correct spelling is ‘Facsimile’, which means an exact copy or reproduction — प्रतिलिपि, हूबहू प्रति.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Loathe.”
questions.explanation: B) Loathe (adjective/verb) – Reluctant, unwilling, averse, hesitant, or disinclined to do something. (अनिच्छुक) Synonym: Reluctant (adjective) – Unwilling and hesitant; disinclined to do something. (अनिच्छुक) • Recondite (adjective): Little known; abstruse or obscure. (गूढ़) • Redoubtable (adjective): Formidable, especially as an opponent. (दुर्जेय) • Resplendent (adjective): Attractive and impressive through being richly colorful. (चमकीला)

questions.question: Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of idioms/phrases. A white elephant
options: A useless possession which is extremely expensive to keep
Someone belonging to a royal family
questions.explanation: B) A white elephant (idiom) – A useless possession which is extremely expensive to keep बेकार लेकिन रख-रखाव में बहुत महँगी वस्तु

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In an era dominated by digital devices, regulating children’s screen time has become an increasingly complex challenge for parents and educators alike. While screens have revolutionized the way information is accessed and shared, excessive usage can have detrimental effects on young minds. Numerous studies have indicated that unregulated screen exposure may contribute to sleep disturbances, reduced academic performance, and impaired social interaction skills. Conversely, when used responsibly, digital tools can foster learning, creativity, and global awareness. Hence, experts advocate for a balanced approach, emphasizing the need for clear guidelines and open dialogue between parents and children. Encouraging offline activities, monitoring content, and setting time limits are some strategies recommended to ensure that technology acts as an aid rather than an impediment to a child’s development. Choose the option that correctly converts this sentence into passive voice: "Experts advocate for a balanced approach."
options: A balanced approach was advocated by experts.
A balanced approach has been advocating by experts.
A balanced approach is advocated for by experts.
questions.explanation: C) A balanced approach is advocated for by experts. Active से Passive Voice में बदलने के लिए, Active वाक्य का Object Passive वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है और Verb को उसी Tense के Passive रूप में बदला जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में "Experts" Subject है, "advocate for" Verb (Simple Present) है और "a balanced approach" Object है। Passive में Object "A balanced approach" Subject बनता है और Verb को is advocated for (Simple Present Passive) में बदला जाता है, साथ में Agent “by experts” जोड़ा जाता है। To change a sentence from Active to Passive Voice, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence and the verb is transformed into the passive form of the same tense. Here, “advocate for” in Simple Present becomes is advocated for in Simple Present Passive, with the agent added using “by.”

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In an era dominated by digital devices, regulating children’s screen time has become an increasingly complex challenge for parents and educators alike. While screens have revolutionized the way information is accessed and shared, excessive usage can have detrimental effects on young minds. Numerous studies have indicated that unregulated screen exposure may contribute to sleep disturbances, reduced academic performance, and impaired social interaction skills. Conversely, when used responsibly, digital tools can foster learning, creativity, and global awareness. Hence, experts advocate for a balanced approach, emphasizing the need for clear guidelines and open dialogue between parents and children. Encouraging offline activities, monitoring content, and setting time limits are some strategies recommended to ensure that technology acts as an aid rather than an impediment to a child’s development. Which of the following sentences from the passage contains a present perfect tense verb?
options: Regulating children's screen time has become an increasingly complex challenge.
Screens have revolutionized the way information is accessed and shared.
Experts advocate for a balanced approach.
Encouraging offline activities, monitoring content, and setting time limits are some strategies recommended.
questions.explanation: A) ‘Regulating children's screen time has become an increasingly complex challenge. has become’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यह present perfect tense की संरचना has + past participle को follow करता है। Sentence में बताया गया है कि regulating children’s screen time अब तक एक complex challenge बन चुका है — यानी यह परिवर्तन past में शुरू हुआ और उसका परिणाम present में भी बना हुआ है। इसलिए, option A सही उत्तर है। • ‘has become’ should be used because it follows the present perfect tense structure (has + past participle). The sentence shows that the challenge developed in the past and continues in the present. Thus, option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In an era dominated by digital devices, regulating children’s screen time has become an increasingly complex challenge for parents and educators alike. While screens have revolutionized the way information is accessed and shared, excessive usage can have detrimental effects on young minds. Numerous studies have indicated that unregulated screen exposure may contribute to sleep disturbances, reduced academic performance, and impaired social interaction skills. Conversely, when used responsibly, digital tools can foster learning, creativity, and global awareness. Hence, experts advocate for a balanced approach, emphasizing the need for clear guidelines and open dialogue between parents and children. Encouraging offline activities, monitoring content, and setting time limits are some strategies recommended to ensure that technology acts as an aid rather than an impediment to a child’s development. Which word is the closest synonym for "impediment" as used in the passage?
questions.explanation: A) Impediment (noun) – something that obstructs, hinders, or slows progress बाधा / अवरोध Synonym: Barrier (noun) – an obstacle that blocks movement or progress रुकावट / अवरोध • Opportunity (noun) – a favorable chance अवसर • Pathway (noun) – a route or way forward मार्ग • Solution (noun) – an answer to a problem समाधान

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In an era dominated by digital devices, regulating children’s screen time has become an increasingly complex challenge for parents and educators alike. While screens have revolutionized the way information is accessed and shared, excessive usage can have detrimental effects on young minds. Numerous studies have indicated that unregulated screen exposure may contribute to sleep disturbances, reduced academic performance, and impaired social interaction skills. Conversely, when used responsibly, digital tools can foster learning, creativity, and global awareness. Hence, experts advocate for a balanced approach, emphasizing the need for clear guidelines and open dialogue between parents and children. Encouraging offline activities, monitoring content, and setting time limits are some strategies recommended to ensure that technology acts as an aid rather than an impediment to a child’s development. What does the word “detrimental” most nearly mean as used in the passage?
questions.explanation: C) Detrimental (adjective) – causing harm, damage, or negative effect हानिकारक / नुक़सानदेह Meaning: Harmful (adjective) – injurious, damaging हानिकारक • Unimportant (adjective) – not significant महत्वहीन • Beneficial (adjective) – helpful, advantageous लाभकारी • Unpredictable (adjective) – not able to be foretold अप्रत्याशित

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question: In an era dominated by digital devices, regulating children’s screen time has become an increasingly complex challenge for parents and educators alike. While screens have revolutionized the way information is accessed and shared, excessive usage can have detrimental effects on young minds. Numerous studies have indicated that unregulated screen exposure may contribute to sleep disturbances, reduced academic performance, and impaired social interaction skills. Conversely, when used responsibly, digital tools can foster learning, creativity, and global awareness. Hence, experts advocate for a balanced approach, emphasizing the need for clear guidelines and open dialogue between parents and children. Encouraging offline activities, monitoring content, and setting time limits are some strategies recommended to ensure that technology acts as an aid rather than an impediment to a child’s development. According to the passage, what is one way to ensure technology benefits children?
options: Avoid any use of digital tools entirely.
Allow unlimited screen time to encourage freedom.
Monitor content and set clear time limits.
Encourage children to use screens late at night.
questions.explanation: C) ‘Monitor content and set clear time limits’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में स्पष्ट रूप से बताया गया है कि technology को बच्चों के विकास के लिए लाभकारी बनाने हेतु strategies जैसे monitoring content और setting time limits recommend की गई हैं। ये उपाय balanced use सुनिश्चित करते हैं। इसलिए, option C सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है। • ‘Monitor content and set clear time limits’ should be used because the passage clearly states that recommended strategies include monitoring content and setting time limits so that technology works as an aid rather than a hindrance. Thus, option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: One of these words is spelled incorrectly. Which one is it?
questions.explanation: C) The word ‘Occassion’ is spelled incorrectly. The correct spelling is ‘Occasion’, which means a particular event or time — अवसर, घटना.

questions.question: Choose the correct one-word substitution for the italicized phrase. “The country plunged into chaos after it was thrown into a state of political instability characterized by the removal of a leader by force.”
questions.explanation: C) Coup (noun) – A sudden and forceful removal of a political leader or government तख़्तापलट • Revolution (noun) – A large-scale and often violent change in government or social order क्रांति • Insurrection (noun) – A violent uprising against authority विद्रोह • Rebellion (noun) – An act of resistance against established authority बगावत

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Heinous.”
questions.explanation: A) Heinous (adjective) – (Of a person or wrongful act, especially a crime) utterly odious, wicked, monstrous, or atrocious. (जघन्य/घृणित) Synonym: Abominable (adjective) – Very bad or unpleasant; causing moral revulsion; detestable or loathsome. (घृणित) • Abstemious (adjective): Not self-indulgent, especially when eating and drinking. (संयमी) • Ambivalent (adjective): Having mixed feelings or contradictory ideas about something. (उभयभावी) • Adventitious (adjective): Happening or carried on according to chance rather than design. (आकस्मिक)

questions.question: Choose the correct passive voice transformation of the sentence: They had not finished the project before the deadline.
options: The project was not finished by them before the deadline.
The project has not been finished by them before the deadline.
The project had not been finished by them before the deadline.
The project was not being finished by them before the deadline.
questions.explanation: C) The project had not been finished by them before the deadline. Active से Passive Voice में बदलने के लिए, Active वाक्य का Object Passive वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है और Verb को उसी Tense के Passive रूप में बदला जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में Tense Past Perfect है। Active वाक्य में “They” Subject है, “had not finished” Verb (Past Perfect) है और “the project” Object है। Passive में Object “the project” Subject बन जाता है और Verb को had not been finished (Past Perfect Passive) में बदला जाता है, जबकि “before the deadline” वाला समय-सूचक वाक्यांश वही रहता है। To change a sentence from Active to Passive Voice, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence, and the verb is converted into its passive form while maintaining the same tense. Here, the sentence is in the Past Perfect tense. “They” is the subject, “had not finished” is the verb, and “the project” is the object. In the passive construction, the correct form is “had not been finished.”

questions.question: The following sentence has two blanks. Choose the most suitable set of prepositions from the given options to fill in the blanks and complete the sentence meaningfully: The politician distanced himself ___ the scandal and shifted blame ___ his aides.
questions.explanation: B) from, onto’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि “distance oneself” के साथ सही preposition from आती है, जो किसी व्यक्ति या विषय से अलग होने को दर्शाती है। वहीं “shift blame” के साथ onto का प्रयोग किया जाता है, जो दोष किसी और पर डालने की क्रिया को स्पष्ट करता है। इसलिए वाक्य में दोनों blanks के लिए from, onto सबसे उपयुक्त हैं। • ‘from, onto’ should be used because “to distance oneself” is correctly followed by from, indicating separation or dissociation from something. Similarly, “to shift blame” takes onto, showing that responsibility is being transferred to another person. Hence, from, onto is the most appropriate set of prepositions.

questions.question: Choose the best option to replace the underlined part to improve the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select 'No substitution.' I watched the paper boat float on the canal towards the lake.
questions.explanation: A) down the canal 'on the canal' के बदले 'down the canal' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि movement with the flow / direction बताने के लिए preposition down प्रयोग किया जाता है; जैसे— The leaves drifted down the river. • 'down the canal' will be used instead of 'on the canal' because it correctly expresses directional movement along the waterway.

questions.question: Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of idioms/phrases. Look before you leap
options: Analyze the consequences before making a decision
Depending on others for task completion
Always be humble to people of lower designation
questions.explanation: B) Look before you leap (idiom) – Analyze the consequences before making a decision किसी भी काम को करने से पहले उसके परिणामों के बारे में सोचना

questions.question: Change the following sentence into the Active form. "The groundbreaking quantum theory should not have been dismissed as mere speculation by the scientific community."
options: The scientific community should not have been dismissing the groundbreaking quantum theory as mere speculation.
The scientific community should not have dismissed the groundbreaking quantum theory as mere speculation.
The groundbreaking quantum theory should not be dismissed as mere speculation by the scientific community.
The scientific community should not be dismissing the groundbreaking quantum theory as mere speculation.
questions.explanation: B) The scientific community should not have dismissed the groundbreaking quantum theory as mere speculation. Passive से Active Voice में बदलने के लिए, Passive वाक्य में प्रयुक्त auxiliary structure (should not have been + V-ing / V³) को हटाकर Verb को Active form में लाया जाता है, जबकि कर्त्ता (doer) वही रखा जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में “should not have been dismissing” एक unnecessarily complex form है, जिसका Active और natural रूप should not have dismissed होता है। Subject “the scientific community” पहले से ही कर्त्ता है, इसलिए उसे बदला नहीं जाता, केवल Verb form को सही Active tense में बदला जाता है। To change a sentence into Active Voice, the subject performing the action is retained, and the verb is simplified into its correct active form while preserving meaning and tense. Here, “should not have been dismissing” is best converted to the active modal perfect form “should not have dismissed,” which is grammatically correct and natural.

questions.question: Which word can best replace groped? His hands groped along the wall for the lamp.
questions.explanation: B) 'groped' के बदले 'fumbled' होगा क्योंकि groped का अर्थ है अंधेरे या बिना देखे हाथों से टटोलना, जो अर्थ के अनुसार fumbled (हाथों से तलाश करना/अटपटा ढंग से खोज करना) से मेल खाता है। • 'fumbled' will be used instead of 'groped' because both mean to search uncertainly by touch, especially when visibility is low.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate preposition: After the heavy rain, the floodwaters rose _____ the level of the streets.
questions.explanation: B) above’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि वाक्य में यह बताया जा रहा है कि भारी बारिश के बाद बाढ़ का पानी सड़कों के स्तर से ऊपर चला गया। यहाँ rose above the level का अर्थ है कि पानी ने सामान्य ऊँचाई को पार कर लिया। इसलिए “above” सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प है। • ‘Above’ should be used because the sentence indicates that after the heavy rain, the floodwaters went higher than the level of the streets. The phrase rose above the level clearly conveys that the water exceeded the normal street height. Thus, “above” is the most appropriate choice.
